summary: Excellent recording with strong early sets and a standout Desolation Row, but hampered by a dull drum segment and weaker second‑set finish.
review: The soundboard capture of this spring ’87 show is praised as excellent, delivering clear detail especially before the drums. Musically the first part shines – inventive “Eyes” and an “Unbelievable Desolation Row” are highlighted – yet the drum solo is called the most boring ever and the latter half of the second set falls short, leaving a mixed overall impression.

ai_rating.rationale: Recording quality is rated excellent while the performance is split – strong early songs but notable weak spots (drums, second‑set tail), leading to an overall moderate rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewer describes the recording as excellent with clear detail before drums; no specific source type mentioned.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First half and early second‑set are inventive and engaging, but the drum solo is dull and the later portion of the second set loses momentum.

band_member_comments.Drums: Described as the most boring drums ever heard on this show.
